fiber:low.rationale: Legumes like lentils are one of the most fiber-dense foods per calorie and drop easily into soups or salads.
Chickpeas add fiber and plant protein in one ingredient.
Whole oats are a simple, versatile source of fiber for breakfast.
Swapping refined bread for whole-grain bread raises fiber without changing your routine.

protein:low.rationale: Greek yogurt is a concentrated, versatile source of protein.
Tofu is a plant-based protein that works in almost any savory dish.
Lentils combine plant protein with fiber.
Eggs are an affordable, complete source of protein.

sugar:high.rationale: Swapping sugary drinks for unsweetened tea or water is the single biggest lever on added sugar.
Plain yogurt has a fraction of the sugar of flavored yogurt.
Nuts make a lower-sugar snack swap for sweets.

processed:high.rationale: Swapping a ready meal for fresh vegetables lowers your basket's average processing level.
Whole-grain staples are less processed than their refined equivalents.
A whole-food snack in place of packaged snacks reduces overall processing.
